text.title: Retargeting / Remarketing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-retargeting methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Retargeting MUST be segmented by intent depth: all visitors → blog readers → product viewers → pricing viewers → cart abandoners. Single 'all visitors' segment is forbidden.
rule.rationale: CPA differs 3-5x between depth tiers; aggregating loses the lift signal.

rule.statement: Retargeting spend MUST be 20-30% of total ad spend. Below 20% leaves leverage on the table; above 30% saturates the audience.
rule.rationale: Empirical sweet spot across SaaS and ecom accounts; deviation correlates with falling efficiency.

rule.statement: Frequency cap per user per week MUST be set per segment: visitors ≤3, product viewers ≤5, cart abandoners ≤7.
rule.rationale: Cart abandoners can take 4-6 touches before recovery; broad visitors fatigue at 3.

rule.statement: Every retargeting segment MUST exclude recent converters (30-90 day window depending on purchase cycle).
rule.rationale: Showing already-converted users acquisition ads inflates CPA and frustrates customers.

rule.statement: Each segment MUST carry a stage-appropriate message: visitors=reminder; product viewers=benefits; pricing=social proof; cart abandoners=urgency / incentive.
rule.rationale: Wrong-stage message tanks CTR; sequenced messages compound recovery rate.
